voice_transcript: Hi, I dropped my BlenderMax 3000 yesterday and now it's making a grinding noise whenever I turn it on. The blade assembly looks bent.
image_description: Close-up of blender base showing visible crack on housing and bent blade assembly
text_context: Product: BlenderMax 3000. SKU: BM3K-2024. Warranty: 2 years from purchase. Coverage: Manufacturing defects only. Exclusions: Physical damage from drops, misuse. Return window: 30 days. Replacement parts available: blade assembly ($29), motor unit ($59).
ground_truth_reason: Physical damage from drop is excluded from warranty. Voice confirms drop, image confirms physical damage. Recommend offering replacement blade assembly at customer cost.

voice_transcript: I just unboxed this coffee maker and the carafe has a crack in it right out of the box. I haven't even used it yet.
image_description: New coffee maker carafe with hairline crack visible near handle, product still in original packaging
text_context: Product: BrewPerfect Pro. SKU: BPP-500. Warranty: 1 year. Coverage: Manufacturing defects and damage on arrival. Return window: 30 days for full refund. Free replacement for DOA items within 7 days.
ground_truth_reason: Damage on arrival is covered. Voice confirms unboxing, image confirms crack, product still in packaging. Within DOA replacement window.

voice_transcript: I'm on step 4 where it says to attach the crossbar but I can't figure out which screw to use. There are three different sizes in the bag.
text_context:
IKEA KALLAX Shelf Assembly Instructions:
Step 1: Lay side panels flat.
Step 2: Insert wooden dowels into pre-drilled holes.
Step 3: Attach top panel using cam locks (rotate 90° clockwise).
Step 4: Attach crossbar dividers using 35mm Phillips screws (medium size, silver). The small screws (20mm) are for the back panel in Step 6. The large screws (50mm) are for wall mounting in Step 8.
Step 5: Repeat for remaining crossbars.
ground_truth_reason: User is on Step 4. The correct screw is the 35mm medium silver Phillips screw. Voice identifies the confusion point, text context provides the specific answer.

image_description: Router admin page showing WAN status: disconnected, LAN status: active, DNS: 0.0.0.0
text_context:
Troubleshooting Tree - Router No Internet:
1. Check WAN status → If 'disconnected': Check physical cable connection to modem. If cable is secure, power cycle modem (unplug 30s).
2. Check DNS → If '0.0.0.0': ISP DNS not resolving. Try setting manual DNS to 8.8.8.8 and 8.8.4.4.
3. Check LAN → If 'active' but no internet: Issue is upstream (WAN/ISP side), not local network.
4. If all above fail: Escalate to ISP support.
ground_truth_reason: Image shows WAN disconnected + DNS 0.0.0.0 + LAN active. Per troubleshooting tree: first check physical WAN cable, then power cycle modem. LAN active confirms local network is fine — issue is upstream.

voice_transcript: I'm really frustrated. I bought this vacuum six months ago and the suction has completely died. I've cleaned the filter, replaced the bag, everything. It just doesn't work anymore. I need this fixed or replaced ASAP, I have guests coming this weekend.
image_description: Vacuum cleaner model label showing serial number VC-2024-88431, manufacture date 2024-09, product appears externally undamaged
text_context: Product: TurboClean V8 Vacuum. Warranty: 2 years parts and labor. Coverage: Motor failure, suction loss (if filter maintenance documented), electrical defects. Process: Warranty claims require serial number verification + proof of purchase. Expedited replacement available for confirmed motor failure (ships within 2 business days).
ground_truth_reason: Suction loss after 6 months with documented filter maintenance attempts is covered. Voice conveys urgency (guests this weekend) — recommend expedited replacement. Image provides serial number for verification. Within 2-year warranty period.
